questions.question: Explain the role of population in the development of resources.
questions.answer: Population plays a key role in developing resources. People discover, use and manage resources through technology and skills.

questions.question: Explain the importance of census in a country.
questions.answer: Census provides accurate data about population size, distribution and characteristics. Governments use it for development planning.

questions.question: Explain the main features of India's population size.
questions.answer: India has a very large population of over 1.21 billion people according to the 2011 Census and it accounts for more than 17% of the world's population.

questions.question: Explain the concept of population distribution with examples.
questions.answer: Population distribution refers to how people are spread across regions. Northern Plains are densely populated while mountainous areas are sparsely populated.

questions.question: Explain why population density varies across India.
questions.answer: Population density varies due to differences in terrain, climate, soil fertility, and economic opportunities.

questions.question: Explain the significance of population density.
questions.answer: Population density helps understand how crowded a region is and how resources and services should be distributed.

questions.question: Explain the meaning and measurement of population growth.
questions.answer: Population growth refers to the increase in population over time and is measured through absolute increase and growth rate.

questions.question: Explain the concept of annual growth rate of population.
questions.answer: Annual growth rate shows the percentage increase in population each year.

questions.question: Explain why India's population increased rapidly after 1951.
questions.answer: India's population increased due to high birth rates and declining death rates resulting from better healthcare.

questions.question: Explain the three main processes of population change.
questions.answer: Population change occurs through birth rate, death rate and migration.

questions.question: Explain the impact of birth rate on population growth.
questions.answer: High birth rate increases population size and contributes to rapid population growth.

questions.question: Explain the impact of death rate on population growth.
questions.answer: A decline in death rate increases population because fewer people die each year.

questions.question: Explain migration and its types.
questions.answer: Migration is the movement of people from one place to another. It can be internal or international migration.

questions.question: Explain rural to urban migration with reasons.
questions.answer: Rural to urban migration occurs due to better employment opportunities, education and living conditions in cities.

questions.question: Explain push factors of migration with examples.
questions.answer: Push factors include poverty, unemployment and lack of facilities in rural areas.

questions.question: Explain pull factors of migration with examples.
questions.answer: Pull factors include better jobs, education and healthcare facilities in urban areas.

questions.question: Explain the growth of urban population in India.
questions.answer: Urban population in India has increased due to migration and better economic opportunities in cities.

questions.question: Explain the concept of million-plus cities.
questions.answer: Million-plus cities are cities with population exceeding one million people.

questions.question: Explain the concept of adolescent population.
questions.answer: Adolescent population refers to people in the age group of 10–19 years.

questions.question: Explain the importance of adolescent population in development.
questions.answer: Adolescents are important because they form the future workforce and contribute to economic development.

questions.question: Explain health challenges faced by adolescents in India.
questions.answer: Adolescents often suffer from poor nutrition and anaemia due to lack of balanced diet.

questions.question: Explain the importance of awareness among adolescent girls.
questions.answer: Awareness helps improve health, education and life opportunities for adolescent girls.

questions.question: Explain the objectives of the Family Planning Programme.
questions.answer: The programme promotes responsible parenthood and helps control population growth.

questions.question: Explain the objectives of National Population Policy 2000.
questions.answer: The policy aims to improve healthcare, education and control population growth.

questions.question: Explain the importance of population planning.
questions.answer: Population planning helps balance population growth with available resources and ensures sustainable development.
